title: Branched conversation with explicit parent_id links

messages.content: What is the best sorting algorithm for nearly-sorted data?
Insertion sort is excellent for nearly-sorted data — it runs in O(n) time when the data is almost sorted, and has very low overhead.
Timsort is the practical answer — it's Python's and Java's default sort, specifically designed to exploit runs of already-sorted elements, giving O(n) best case.
Does insertion sort work well for large datasets too?
